methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: VulcanForms
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Late Stage)

data.narrative:
Kinova Robotics is a late-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Charles Deguire and Louis-Joseph L'Ecuyer. The company has raised $51.6M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes nine comparable companies: one public entity (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private firms, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Stage-progression analysis is applicable and forms the basis of valuation. Using total capital raised ($52M) as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for late-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $62M. Peer-median analysis cannot be applied due to insufficient narrow peer coverage. While seven robotics and manufacturing peers exist sector-wide, none satisfy same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier filters needed for reliable comparison. A broader sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given heterogeneity in robotics subsectors and company maturity.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $53M (low) to $71M (high), with a median point of $62M. The wide range reflects reliance on a single methodology rather than multi-method confirmation. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable late-stage robotics companies with disclosed valuations and the appropriateness of the 1.2x multiplier to Kinova's specific growth trajectory and market positioning. Additional revenue, profitability, or customer concentration data would strengthen conviction in this range.
